thought_beat_text: The seams are honesty—he's almost convinced himself—but honesty that can't be spoken is just another kind of cardboard pretense, and he can feel the fingerprint of that lie pressing into the clay of what he's allowed to see.
residue_summary: The seams mean honesty, but only if he never names them aloud; the permission to see without speaking is suffocating him.
image_hint: stage light catching the foam-core edge, making it impossible to look away
audio_hint: low hum of stage machinery with a sharp, barely-audible creak of pressure
